Plaintiff Logan Dennis' Notice Of Motion To Compel Verified Responses From Defendant City And County Of San Francisco To First Set Of Form Interrogatories (No. 15.1), Motion To Compel Response From Defendant City And County Of San Francisco To First Supplemental Interrogatory And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant City And County Of San Francisco And Their Counsel Vogl Meredith Burke Streza Llp
Off Calendar Aug-31-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: PLAINTIFF LOGAN DENNIS Plaintiff Logan Dennis' Notice Of Motion To Compel Verified Responses From Defendant City And County Of San Francisco To First Set Of Form Interrogatories (No. 15.1), Motion To Compel Response From Defendant City And County Of San Francisco To First Supplemental Interrogatory And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant City And County Of San Francisco And Their Counsel Vogl Meredith Burke Streza Llp.

Appearances:

Gregory M. Sheffer, Esq., Sheffer Law Firm, (415) 388-0911, for Plaintiff.

David Streza, Esq., VOGL MEREDITH BURKE & STREZA LLP, (415) 398-0200, for Defendants.

Both sides previously stipulated to the matter being decided by the pro tem Judge.

The matters are discussed. At conclusion of discussion, the court confirms its tentative ruling from the previous hearing. All motions denied. The deadlines for discovery and discovery motions have long passed as the date initially set for trial in this matter was May 22, 2023. C.C.P. Sec. 2024.020. Fairmont Insurance Company v. Superior Court, 22 Cal.4th 245, (2000). Pelton-Shepherd Industries, Inc. v. Delta Packaging Products, Inc., 165 Cal. App. 4th 1568 (2008); Cottini v. Enloe Med. Ctr., 226 Cal. App. 4th 401 (2014). Since there is no evidence of agreement among the parties to extend discovery deadlines pursuant to C.C.P. Sec. 2024.060 and there is no pending or concurrent motion to reopen/extend discovery and discovery motion deadlines pursuant to C.C.P. Sec. 2024.050, the Court has no choice but to deny the motion. Pelton-Shepherd Industries, Inc. v. Delta Packaging Products, Inc., 165 Cal. App. 4th 1568 (2008).

Judge Pro Tem: William C. Lynn. Clerk: R. Woods. Court Reporter: Vienna Nguyen, CSR: 13137, T: (949) 295-5737, E: viennanguyen@gmail.com. (302/JPT)
